Tests for the `Math.min` function in JavaScript, covering various argument scenarios and properties.


## Files
- **[15.08.02.12-001.js](15.08.02.12-001.js.md)**: Tests the behavior of `Math.min` with `undefined` as an argument.
- **[15.08.02.12-002.js](15.08.02.12-002.js.md)**: Tests the behavior of `Math.min` when given an object as an argument.
- **[15.08.02.12-003.js](15.08.02.12-003.js.md)**: Tests that `Math.min` returns `NaN` when given an empty object as an argument.
- **[15.08.02.12-004.js](15.08.02.12-004.js.md)**: Tests that `Math.min` returns `NaN` when given `NaN` as an argument.
- **[15.08.02.12-005.js](15.08.02.12-005.js.md)**: Tests the behavior of Math.min function when one of the arguments is NaN.
- **[15.08.02.12-006.js](15.08.02.12-006.js.md)**: Tests that `Math.min` returns `NaN` when one of its arguments is `NaN`.
- **[15.08.02.12-007.js](15.08.02.12-007.js.md)**: Tests the Math.min function to verify it returns the smallest of the given numbers.
- **[15.08.02.12-008.js](15.08.02.12-008.js.md)**: Tests the behavior of `Math.min()` with no arguments to ensure it returns `Infinity`.
- **[15.08.02.12-009.js](15.08.02.12-009.js.md)**: Tests that `Math.min()` returns `Infinity` when called with no arguments.
- **[15.08.02.12-010.js](15.08.02.12-010.js.md)**: Tests that `Math.min()` without arguments does not return `-Infinity`.
- **[15.08.02.12-011.js](15.08.02.12-011.js.md)**: Tests the behavior of Math.min with positive and negative zero.
- **[15.08.02.12-012.js](15.08.02.12-012.js.md)**: Tests if `Math.min` is a function.
- **[15.08.02.12-013.js](15.08.02.12-013.js.md)**: Tests that the `Math.min` function has a length property equal to 2.
- **[15.08.02.12-014.js](15.08.02.12-014.js.md)**: Tests the behavior of `Math.min` with positive and negative zero in JavaScript.
